text:
CAD Training
North County Transit District
(NCTD)

Train Management and Dispatch System (TMDS)
Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) Training

text_items: To Start TMDS double-click on the desktop icon.
Then verify that the Title Bar shows the correct version.
text:
Starting CAD
Starting TMDS
To Start TMDS double-click on the desktop icon.
Then verify that the Title Bar shows the correct version.

text_items: Select User from the drop-down menu
text:
Starting CAD
Starting TMDS
To log on:
On menu bar CONTROL
 SYSTEM LOG ON
Select User from the drop-down menu
Enter user password

text_items: Right-Click on territory you wish to control. Multiple territories can be selected.
Note: To remove territories from the control frame, click the CLEAR button. Individual territories cannot be cleared.
text:
Starting CAD
Selecting a Territory
After credentials are accepted:
Right-Click on territory you wish to control. Multiple territories can be selected.
The click Load
Note: To remove territories from the control frame, click the CLEAR button. Individual territories cannot be cleared.

text_items: When CAD loads you will have two screens: The track line display (TLD) and the System Summary.
Note: If more than one territory is selected, each territory will have it’s own window.
On multiple monitor set ups these can moved to desired monitor.
text:
When CAD loads you will have two screens: The track line display (TLD) and the System Summary.
Note: If more than one territory is selected, each territory will have it’s own window.
On multiple monitor set ups these can moved to desired monitor.

text:
Logging Off CAD:
CONTROL

Log Off – Locks the workstation.
Screen greys out and no dispatcher
functionality is available. Useful for breaks or
stepping away from workstation.
Release Territory – Releases the territory under DS control. An authorized user can then sign into that territory
Exit System – Once territory released,
then Exit System is available
Logging On/Off

After logging off:
Only password is needed
After releasing territory:
Full log in needed
To log back on:

To Exit System:
After releasing the territory
CONTROL
 EXIT SYSTEM

Control Drop-Down menu:
Log Off – Locks the workstation.
Screen greys out and no dispatcher
functionality is available. Useful for breaks or
stepping away from workstation.
Release Territory – Releases the territory under DS control. An authorized user can then sign into that territory
Exit System – Once territory released,
then Exit System is available

text_items: Left limit of a currently selected track segment
Right limit of a currently selected track
Name of the user logged in and having control of the territory
The X coordinate of the current mouse cursor position on the track line display. The left side X = 0, and the right X = 1898
The Y coordinates the current mouse cursor position on the track line display. The top Y = 0, and the bottom Y = 1073
The time and date from the system clock
The number of handles the application is currently using
The amount of computer memory that the application is currently using
This shows that the workstation is connected to the database server
text:
Track Line Display
Status Bar
LL:
Left limit of a currently selected track segment
RL:
Right limit of a currently selected track
USER:
Name of the user logged in and having control of the territory
X:
The X coordinate of the current mouse cursor position on the track line display. The left side X = 0, and the right X = 1898
Y:
The Y coordinates the current mouse cursor position on the track line display. The top Y = 0, and the bottom Y = 1073
Date and time:
The time and date from the system clock
H:
The number of handles the application is currently using
M:
The amount of computer memory that the application is currently using
CONNECTED TO:
This shows that the workstation is connected to the database server
FEEDBACK:
Shows feedback to the dispatcher

text_items: Right-click on the black background area within the track line display window and a General Functions (GF) menu will be displayed.
text:
TMDS Functions
General Functions Menu
Right-click on the black background area within the track line display window and a General Functions (GF) menu will be displayed.

text_items: Right-clicking while the cursor is on a track segment, signal, switch point, or control point of any window containing a track line display causes the DC menu to appear.
text:
TMDS Functions
Dynamic Context Menu
Right-clicking while the cursor is on a track segment, signal, switch point, or control point of any window containing a track line display causes the DC menu to appear.

text_items: Enter Issue a Restrictive Information Tag Mode
Enter Position a Train ID Mode
Displays Alias ID for train symbol
Displays Symbol identifier for train symbol
Rotate through stacked track line screen will be displayed
Toggles between Fast Route Mode and Stack Route Mode. Fast Route Mode is the default option. The cursor is signal with arrow – Stack Rote Mode cursor is same but with “STACK” under the signal.
text:
TMDS Functions
Hot Keys and Shortcuts
F4:
Enter Issue a Restrictive Information Tag Mode
F5:
Enter Issue T&T Mode
F7:
Enter Device
Blocl
Create mode
F9:
Enter Position a Train ID Mode
F11:
Train ID Displa
y Option Toggle
CTRL+A:
Displays Alias ID for train symbol
CTRL+E:
Engine IDs Displayed
CTRL+G:
Toggle Signals On/Off
CTRL+H:
Toggle Crossings On/Off
CTRL+I:
Instant Message
CTRL+L:
Log On/Off
CTRL+P:
Displays Train Symbol
Psuedo
IDs
CTRL+Q:
Toggle Fast Route Mode On/Off
CTRL+S:
Displays Symbol identifier for train symbol
CTRL+F6:
Rotate through stacked track line screen will be displayed
CTRL+F12:
Toggles between Fast Route Mode and Stack Route Mode. Fast Route Mode is the default option. The cursor is signal with arrow – Stack Rote Mode cursor is same but with “STACK” under the signal.

text_items: Hovering over a track segment, switch (point), or signal causes a tooltip to be displayed that provides information about that element.

Hovering over a track segment containing bulleting restrictions, device blocks, track tags, and/or track and time authorities will provide information about those, along with the track segment information.
text:
TMDS Functions
Tool Tips
Hovering over a track segment, switch (point), or signal causes a tooltip to be displayed that provides information about that element.
Hovering over a track segment containing bulleting restrictions, device blocks, track tags, and/or track and time authorities will provide information about those, along with the track segment information.
TO THE CAD DISPLAY!

text_items: TMDS displays an alert banner along the bottom of the primary track line screen whenever the dispatcher needs to be alerted to a situation that requires his/her attention.
Clicking on the alert banner brings the ALERT tab of the SYSTEM SUMMARY to the foreground.
text:
TMDS Functions
ALERT BANNERS
TMDS displays an alert banner along the bottom of the primary track line screen whenever the dispatcher needs to be alerted to a situation that requires his/her attention.
Clicking on the alert banner brings the ALERT tab of the SYSTEM SUMMARY to the foreground.

text_items: – Contains information about speed restrictions that must be communicated to a. maintenance personnel and train crews who enter the speed restriction's limits.

– Contains information about a failure or malfunction of a highway crossing safety device that must be communicated to a. maintenance personnel and b. any train crew before they reach that crossing location.

– Contains information and reminders that are useful to the dispatcher regarding conditions other than a speed restriction or highway crossing protection system issue and which may need to be conveyed to maintenance personnel or a train crew.
text:
TMDS Functions
Track Tags:
Speed
– Contains information about speed restrictions that must be communicated to a. maintenance personnel and train crews who enter the speed restriction's limits.
Highway Xing (Crossing)
– Contains information about a failure or malfunction of a highway crossing safety device that must be communicated to a. maintenance personnel and b. any train crew before they reach that crossing location.
Informational
– Contains information and reminders that are useful to the dispatcher regarding conditions other than a speed restriction or highway crossing protection system issue and which may need to be conveyed to maintenance personnel or a train crew.

text_items: When a device block is applied on a track, the track is colored lavender
If the dispatcher attempts to line a signal through the track block, the feedback message will display a
message, and the route request will be canceled.
text:
TMDS Functions
Device Blocks:
When a device block is applied on a track, the track is colored lavender
If the dispatcher attempts to line a signal through the track block, the feedback message will display a
BLOCK IN ROUTE
message, and the route request will be canceled.
